author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session focused on reviewing the Chief of Staff (CoS) agent's task delegation behavior, running a logic inspector audit of the pipeline, and then evaluating and implementing a set of pipeline fixes. Explored how CoS delegates to other agents and skills. After the audit surfaced several issues, implemented fixes targeting pipeline stability. Also began exploring the appPackage/declarative agent configuration. Discussed the idea of creating a separate local-only branch without Bedrock for local testing. Session wrapped up with Ameen asking GitHub Copilot to take over for certain tasks.
problems: CoS agent's delegation to other agents and skills was unclear and needed investigation
Multiple pipeline issues found by logic inspector: bugs, inconsistencies, and improvement opportunities
No easy way to run sessions locally without Bedrock (local runner used a stub)
GitHub Actions runner was failing to acquire a hosted runner in the enterprise EMU environment
approaches: Investigated how CoS delegates tasks to other agents and skills
Ran logic inspector audit on pipeline critical files including decision.md and status.md
Performed QA on audit findings to verify they were accurate before implementing fixes
Created plan and started implementing pipeline fixes
Evaluated the overall project/pipeline and got an honest assessment
Explored adding a local-only branch without Bedrock for offline testing
results: Logic inspector audit completed and findings QA'd
Pipeline rated honestly with known weaknesses documented
Bedrock issue acknowledged as a non-determinism concern with no simple fix yet
Decided to hand off remaining tasks to GitHub Copilot for continuation

decisions.decision: CoS should recommend the LLM model to use for the next task so Ameen can switch manually
A separate branch without Bedrock (local-only) would be useful for local testing

open_questions: Which specific pipeline fixes were implemented and what tests pass after them?
Is a separate no-Bedrock branch feasible without diverging too much from main?
